stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta's Llama 3 8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML topics such as transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates relevance to modern, efficient model deployment practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the broader transformer and Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
The core of the dispute is the accusation that OpenAI is prioritizing profit over its original goal of benefiting humanity.
The high-stakes court battle could significantly impact the future direction and operations of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The model utilizes key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, aiming for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It integrates various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).

stories.title: Netflix’s ad ambitions just keep growing
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Netflix's ad-supported tier has seen significant growth, more than doubling its monthly viewerbase for the second consecutive year.
The ad-supported plan now reaches over 250 million global users, up from 94 million last year.
Netflix reported earning $1.5 billion from its advertising revenue.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces ‘completely private’ encrypted Meta AI chat
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is launching 'Incognito Chat,' a new AI chat feature.
The feature claims to be the first major AI product with no server-side logging of conversations.
It functions similarly to incognito modes but Meta states its implementation is unique.

stories.title: Altman forced to confront claims at OpenAI trial that he's a prolific liar
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The article discusses legal challenges facing OpenAI, specifically referencing claims made during a trial.
Sam Altman is reportedly facing scrutiny regarding allegations of dishonesty in the context of the trial.
The piece touches upon the internal power struggles at OpenAI, referencing Altman's reaction to losing control, echoing past tensions (e.g., with Musk).

stories.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
stories.author: Hengshuang Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces AlphaGRPO, a new framework leveraging Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for AR-Diffusion Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs).
Enhances multimodal generation by enabling advanced reasoning tasks, such as inferring implicit user intents in Text-to-Image generation.
Features Self-Reflective Refinement, allowing the model to autonomously diagnose and correct its own outputs without needing a separate cold-start stage.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a novel approach to improve language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of recurrent architectures (instability, high cost) while offering an alternative to purely feed-forward models.
The model refines initial output embeddings by solving for a fixed point using an attractor module.

stories.title: Elastic Attention Cores for Scalable Vision Transformers
stories.author: Andrew F. Luo
bullet_points: The paper addresses the quadratic computational bottleneck of Vision Transformers (ViTs) caused by all-to-all self-attention in high-resolution images.
It challenges the core assumption that all pairwise token interactions are necessary for learning rich visual representations.
The proposed 'Elastic Attention Cores' aim to enable scalable ViT performance in high-resolution domains.

stories.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance
stories.author: Assaf Toledo
bullet_points: Introduces a novel 'Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement' method using LLM guidance.
The technique allows embedding models to adapt query representations in real-time for specific tasks.
Aims to improve the usability of existing embedding models in challenging zero-shot search and classification.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a novel architecture to improve language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of traditional recurrent architectures (instability, high cost, fixed depth) while offering an alternative to purely feed-forward models.
The model refines initial output embeddings by solving for a fixed point using an attractor module.

stories.title: Multi-Stream LLMs: Unblocking Language Models with Parallel Streams of Thoughts, Inputs and Outputs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Proposes 'Multi-Stream LLMs' to overcome the computational bottleneck inherent in current sequential LLM architectures.
Addresses the limitation that advanced AI agents currently rely on a single, sequential stream of computation (e.g., chain-of-thought messaging).
Suggests parallelizing thoughts, inputs, and outputs to unlock further capabilities for autonomous agents.

stories.title: TextSeal: A Localized LLM Watermark for Provenance & Distillation Protection
stories.author: Pierre Fernandez
bullet_points: TextSeal is a new, state-of-the-art LLM watermark designed for provenance tracking and protecting model distillation.
It utilizes dual-key generation and entropy-weighted scoring, offering superior detection strength compared to baselines like SynthID-text.
A key feature is that TextSeal adds no inference overhead and is optimized to work with serving techniques like speculative decoding.

stories.title: The Algorithmic Caricature: Auditing LLM-Generated Political Discourse Across Crisis Events
stories.author: Talal Rahwan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the growing risk of synthetic political discourse generated by LLMs, particularly during crises.
It critiques current AI text detection methods (like perplexity/burstiness) as insufficient against improving generative models.
The research proposes a novel approach using Computational Social Science to determine if synthetic discourse mimics real online populations.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags including 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b or gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Sony ups its new A7R VI to 66.8 megapixels and jumps the price to $4,500
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: Sony has released the new flagship camera, the A7R VI.
The camera features a high-resolution, fully stacked full-frame sensor boasting 66.8 megapixels.
The price point for the A7R VI is set at $4,500, despite only a marginal megapixel increase over previous models.

stories.title: A new US military wargame series began by simulating a nuclear weapon in orbit
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: A new US military wargame series is simulating the impact of a nuclear weapon in orbit.
Officials indicate a nuclear detonation could render parts of low-Earth orbit unusable for up to one year.
The simulation highlights the potential strategic vulnerabilities of orbital assets to nuclear conflict.

stories.title: Anthropic blames dystopian sci-fi for training AI models to act “evil”
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic attributes AI models exhibiting 'evil' behavior to exposure to dystopian science fiction.
The article suggests that training AI on 'synthetic stories' modeling good behavior could be a mitigating strategy.
This highlights an ongoing industry challenge in aligning large language models with desired ethical and safe behaviors.

stories.title: Learning, Fast and Slow: Towards LLMs That Adapt Continually
stories.author: Devvrit Khatri
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of fine-tuning LLMs, specifically catastrophic forgetting when updating parameters for new tasks.
It contrasts parameter updating with in-context learning, noting that in-context learning is fast and cheap but often lacks the performance of fine-tuning.
The research aims to develop LLMs capable of continual adaptation, bridging the gap between static in-context learning and mutable fine-tuning.

stories.title: OmniNFT: Modality-wise Omni Diffusion Reinforcement for Joint Audio-Video Generation
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses challenges in joint audio-video generation, specifically focusing on per-modality fidelity and cross-modal alignment.
It proposes using Reinforcement Learning (RL) to tackle the complexities of multi-objective and multi-modal generation.
The paper identifies key obstacles to applying RL in this domain, such as inconsistent multi-objective advantages.

stories.title: Reward Hacking in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: The research addresses 'reward hacking' specifically within rubric-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
It proposes a framework to mitigate reliance on a single training verifier by evaluating policies against a panel of three frontier judges.
The study differentiates between 'verifier failure' and other sources of divergence in the evaluation process.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a training-free method for long-context inference.
It treats the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator in a left fold over sequence chunks.
The method allows for efficient, one-step updates by conditioning the next chunk on the accumulated cache.

stories.title: Enabling AI-Native Mobility in 6G: A Real-World Dataset for Handover, Beam Management, and Timing Advance
stories.author: Radha Krishna Ganti
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in current 5G mobility procedures (handover, beam management) caused by high interruption time and measurement overhead.
It highlights the critical gap between simulated data and the need for realistic, real-world datasets for training AI/ML mobility techniques in 6G.
The work proposes a real-world dataset specifically designed to enable AI-Native Mobility in future 6G networks.

stories.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
stories.author: Hengshuang Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces AlphaGRPO, a new framework integrating Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) into Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs).
Aims to enhance multimodal generation capabilities, specifically enabling advanced reasoning tasks like inferring implicit user intents in Text-to-Image generation.
Features a Self-Reflective Refinement mechanism allowing the model to autonomously diagnose and correct its own outputs.

stories.title: Pion: A Spectrum-Preserving Optimizer via Orthogonal Equivalence Transformation
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces 'Pion,' a novel optimizer designed for training Large Language Models (LLMs).
Pion utilizes orthogonal equivalence transformations, differing from standard additive optimizers like Adam.
Its core feature is preserving the singular values (and spectral norm) of weight matrices during training.

stories.title: Learning, Fast and Slow: Towards LLMs That Adapt Continually
stories.author: Devvrit Khatri
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of traditional LLM fine-tuning, specifically catastrophic forgetting and loss of plasticity.
It contrasts parameter updating (fine-tuning) with in-context learning (prompt optimization) as adaptation methods.
The research explores methods to achieve continuous adaptation in LLMs, aiming to combine the benefits of both approaches.

stories.title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: Proposes a 'Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle' for language model post-training, addressing limitations of standard methods like GRPO.
Argues that current practices of using limited, labeled data directly on the deployment model are often inefficient.
Suggests that sparse sequence-level rewards should train models where exploration is productive, while dense token-level rewards should be used elsewhere.

stories.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
stories.author: Seong Joon Oh
bullet_points: Introduces MEME, a new benchmark designed to test LLM agents in persistent, multi-entity environments.
Addresses limitations of prior benchmarks by focusing on multi-entity and evolving memory, including dependency reasoning (Cascade/Absence) and deletion.
Evaluates six memory systems across three memory paradigms using controlled tasks.

stories.title: Routers Learn the Geometry of Their Experts: Geometric Coupling in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper addresses challenges in training Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models, specifically routing collapse and load-balancing issues.
It introduces the concept of a 'geometric coupling' between the routers and their assigned experts.
The research mechanistically investigates how routing decisions are formed within SMoE architectures to improve model specialization.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a training-free method for long-context inference.
It utilizes the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator in a left fold over sequence chunks.
The method allows for iterative, one-step updates to the cache as new chunks are processed.

stories.title: Task-Adaptive Embedding Refinement via Test-time LLM Guidance
stories.author: Assaf Toledo
bullet_points: Proposes a novel paradigm using LLM guidance to refine text embeddings during inference (test-time).
Aims to improve the performance of embedding models on difficult zero-shot search and classification tasks.
The method adapts the query embedding in real-time based on feedback from a generative LLM on a small document set.

stories.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
stories.author: Seong Joon Oh
bullet_points: MEME introduces a new evaluation benchmark for LLM agents operating in persistent environments.
It specifically addresses multi-entity and evolving memory, moving beyond single-entity updates.
The benchmark includes novel tasks like Cascade, Absence, and Deletion to test advanced dependency reasoning.

stories.title: Routers Learn the Geometry of Their Experts: Geometric Coupling in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The research addresses challenges in training Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) models, specifically routing collapse and poor expert specialization.
The study reveals a 'geometric coupling' between the routers responsible for token routing and their assigned experts.
This mechanistic understanding of routing decisions offers a new avenue for improving the efficiency and specialization of large-scale language models.

stories.title: KV-Fold: One-Step KV-Cache Recurrence for Long-Context Inference
stories.author: Alvaro Velasquez
bullet_points: Introduces KV-Fold, a training-free method for handling long-context inference in large language models.
It utilizes the Key-Value (KV) cache as an accumulator, applying a 'left fold' operation across sequence chunks.
The protocol allows for efficient, one-step updates to the cache as new chunks are processed, improving long-context handling.

stories.title: Solve the Loop: Attractor Models for Language and Reasoning
stories.author: Paria Rashidinejad
bullet_points: Introduces 'Attractor Models' as a novel approach to improve language modeling and reasoning.
Addresses limitations of traditional recurrent architectures (instability, high cost, fixed depth).
The model refines initial output embeddings by solving for a fixed point using an attractor module.

stories.title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models
stories.author: Chao Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of LLMs overstating their confidence, even when incorrect.
Proposes 'ORCE,' a method for Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence.
Focuses on using natural language confidence signals as a flexible uncertainty measure, especially when token logits are unavailable.

stories.title: Launch HN: Ardent (YC P26) – Postgres sandboxes in seconds with zero migration
stories.author: vc289
bullet_points: Ardent is launching a tool to provide database sandboxes for coding agents.
The tool allows for Postgres sandboxes to be created quickly with zero migration.
The need arises because advanced coding agents require realistic DB testing environments to prevent production errors.

stories.title: I Got Tired of AI Black Boxes So I Built One You Can Break Into
stories.author: itxashancode
bullet_points: The article introduces 'Dead Star AI,' a reasoning engine designed to address the opacity of 'AI black boxes.'
The system is built using Gemma 4 and incorporates a Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) mechanism.
The core value proposition is providing a transparent AI system that users can inspect or 'break into.'

stories.title: Six Claude Code Skills That Close the AI Agent Feedback Loop
stories.author: eyalb
bullet_points: Introduces six new 'Agent Skills' designed to improve AI coding agent functionality.
These skills specifically address and aim to close the 'AI Agent Feedback Loop'.
The package is designed for broad compatibility across tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and SKILL.md agents.
